channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: 2 young people arrested in alleged plot to attack Houston synagogue
item.description: Two young people have been arrested in an alleged plot to attack a Texas synagogue that involved driving through the congregation to "kill as many Jews as possible," according authorities.
item.content:encoded:
Two young people have been arrested in an alleged plot to attack a Texas synagogue that involved driving through the congregation to "kill as many Jews as possible," according authorities.

(Image credit: Paul Sancya)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: U.S. soldier charged with using classified information to bet on Maduro's removal
item.description: It's the first time suspected insider trading on Polymarket has led to criminal charges in the U.S.
item.content:encoded:
It's the first time suspected insider trading on Polymarket has led to criminal charges in the U.S.

(Image credit: Jesus Vargas)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: Trump administration moves to reschedule medical marijuana
item.description: The Trump administration is easing restrictions on medical marijuana. The acting attorney general issued a memo saying he is moving the cannabis products out of the most restrictive category.
item.content:encoded: The Trump administration is easing restrictions on medical marijuana. The acting attorney general issued a memo saying he is moving the cannabis products out of the most restrictive category.
item.dc:creator: Will Stone

item.title: Stolen book of John Keats' love letters are returned to their rightful owner
item.description: Nearly 40 years ago, a book containing eight letters John Keats wrote to his fiancee Fanny Brawne disappeared. They resurfaced last year and, this week, returned to their rightful owner.
item.content:encoded: Nearly 40 years ago, a book containing eight letters John Keats wrote to his fiancee Fanny Brawne disappeared. They resurfaced last year and, this week, returned to their rightful owner.
item.dc:creator: Marc Rivers

item.title: Can restricting abortion reverse Wyoming's long-standing population drain?
item.description: Backers of Wyoming's under-litigation abortion restrictions say they will help reverse a dwindling state population. Critics say it won't stop kids from leaving the economically challenged state.
item.content:encoded: Backers of Wyoming's under-litigation abortion restrictions say they will help reverse a dwindling state population. Critics say it won't stop kids from leaving the economically challenged state.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Merzbach

item.title: Author details the spy network that took on America's post-WWII Nazi groups
item.description: In The Secret War Against Hate, Steven J. Ross details the racist, anti-Semitic groups that sprang up in the latter half of the 20th century — and the spy network that worked to bring them to justice.
item.content:encoded: In The Secret War Against Hate, Steven J. Ross details the racist, anti-Semitic groups that sprang up in the latter half of the 20th century — and the spy network that worked to bring them to justice.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross

item.title: Can a mentalist trick Trump? Oz Pearlman will try in a room full of journalists
item.description: The White House Correspondents' Dinner will be headlined by a mentalist instead of a comedian. Oz Pearlman tells NPR he hopes to unify, delight and puzzle the crowd — but can't reveal how.
item.content:encoded:
The White House Correspondents' Dinner will be headlined by a mentalist instead of a comedian. Oz Pearlman tells NPR he hopes to unify, delight and puzzle the crowd — but can't reveal how.

(Image credit: Arturo Holmes)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: Trump administration eases rules on some marijuana categories. Here's what to know
item.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche said he is immediately moving medical marijuana from Schedule I to Schedule III, which includes drugs like ketamine, Tylenol with codeine and anabolic steroids.
item.content:encoded:
Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche said he is immediately moving medical marijuana from Schedule I to Schedule III, which includes drugs like ketamine, Tylenol with codeine and anabolic steroids.

(Image credit: Justin Sullivan)
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell

item.title: Trump announces a 3-week extension of the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire
item.description: President Trump said Thursday that Israel and Lebanon have agreed to extend their ceasefire for three weeks after the two sides met at the White House for a high-level negotiation.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump said Thursday that Israel and Lebanon have agreed to extend their ceasefire for three weeks after the two sides met at the White House for a high-level negotiation.

(Image credit: Mohammed Zaatari)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Wildfires — fueled by drought — destroy homes across parts of Georgia and Florida
item.description: Drought conditions are fueling wildfires in South Georgia and Northern Florida that have destroyed homes and caused air quality issues across the region.
item.content:encoded: Drought conditions are fueling wildfires in South Georgia and Northern Florida that have destroyed homes and caused air quality issues across the region.
item.dc:creator: Emily Jones
